jokes.text: Wow! Look at the beak on that bird.
jokes.setup: Michael discovers a photo of a possible sister
jokes.punchline: He focuses on her prominent nose rather than the family implications
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - deflecting emotional moments with shallow observations. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: A deformed neighbor or... A child that Mom and Dad gave up for some reason, and whose identity they're now using to pin their crimes on.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain the mystery photo rationally
jokes.punchline: Immediately jumps to the Bluths using an abandoned child as a criminal scapegoat
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the Bluth family's casual criminality and Michael's paranoid worldview. Dark and specific to this family.

jokes.text: Yeah, but that was retard... misguided.
jokes.setup: Michael defending his Mr. F paranoia
jokes.punchline: Catches himself using outdated terminology and awkwardly self-corrects
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's attempt to be PC while revealing his casual insensitivity. The awkward correction is very in-character.

jokes.text: Oh, there's the woman I'm sexually attracted to.
jokes.setup: Lindsay enters the scene
jokes.punchline: Tobias announces his attraction to his own wife with clinical detachment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias - treating his marriage like a clinical study. The formal announcement of attraction to his own wife is absurdly detached.

jokes.text: Yes. Lindsay and I are planning a night of heterosexual intercourse.
jokes.setup: Explaining their romantic plans
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily specifies 'heterosexual' and uses clinical terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias overthinking and over-specifying. The 'heterosexual' qualifier hints at his repressed sexuality without being explicit.

jokes.text: You can just say intercourse.
jokes.setup: Tobias's overly specific sexual terminology
jokes.punchline: Michael's exasperated correction showing even 'intercourse' is too clinical
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Michael's deadpan correction highlights how unnecessarily formal Tobias is being.

jokes.text: I tried that. It didn't work either.
jokes.setup: Lindsay saying she wants him thinking of others during sex
jokes.punchline: Tobias reveals he already tried this approach and failed
jokes.explanation: Reveals their sexual dysfunction through Tobias's matter-of-fact admission of failure. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, you know what? I've already prepared a list of 'won'ts.' / Oh, you're not going to believe this. My list of 'can'ts.'
jokes.setup: Preparing for their romantic evening
jokes.punchline: Both have extensive lists of sexual restrictions, but frame them differently
jokes.explanation: Perfect parallel structure showing their mutual sexual dysfunction. The distinction between 'won't' and 'can't' adds character depth.

jokes.text: We're married?
jokes.setup: George Michael discovers their mock wedding was real
jokes.punchline: His shocked realization of accidental incestuous marriage
jokes.explanation: Sets up the George Michael/Maeby marriage subplot. His innocent shock plays well.

jokes.text: as real and binding as the rice pudding the disoriented patients had thrown at them
jokes.setup: Describing how real their marriage was
jokes.punchline: Comparing legal binding to rice pudding binding, while revealing the 'rice' was actually pudding
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'binding' while adding absurd detail about mental patients throwing pudding instead of rice.

jokes.text: Oh, please. Pity the fool who gives me a bunch of jibber-jabber about romance.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about romance
jokes.punchline: Mr. T's character dismissing romance using his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good character integration of Mr. T's voice, but relies on pop culture reference without much twist.

jokes.text: I mean, if someone was always asking you to embrace the Holy Trinity, what would you think they meant?
jokes.setup: Gob explaining his religious misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Sets up that he interpreted 'Holy Trinity' sexually
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob - misinterpreting religious concepts through his sexual lens. Shows his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Gob thought it meant a three-way.
jokes.setup: Gob's question about the Holy Trinity
jokes.punchline: Narrator explicitly states Gob's sexual misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Clean punchline delivery by narrator. The matter-of-fact explanation of Gob's misunderstanding is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Sister? Uh... No, I don't. Not really... ringing any bells. Other than Lindsay, of course. Oh! Lindsay!
jokes.setup: Michael asking about remembering a sister
jokes.punchline: Gob genuinely forgets Lindsay exists until prompted
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's self-absorption - he literally forgets his sister exists. The delayed recognition is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Oh, Lindsay and Tobias never visit. / Oh, we're so bad!
jokes.setup: Michael asking about two daughters visiting
jokes.punchline: Lucille misunderstands, thinks he meant Tobias as their other 'daughter'
jokes.explanation: Lucille's casual emasculation of Tobias while completely missing Michael's point. Shows her character and hints at Tobias's sexuality.

jokes.text: You call him that, too? It's so nice to be able to talk like this.
jokes.setup: Michael mentions 'Nellie' referring to his mystery sister
jokes.punchline: Lucille thinks he's also calling Tobias 'Nellie' and is delighted
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection. Lucille's joy at sharing Tobias mockery while Michael seeks real information. Perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Think of it as a giant delete key.
jokes.setup: Explaining the electromagnet
jokes.punchline: Casually describing evidence destruction in computer terms
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor showing George Sr.'s casual approach to crime. The tech metaphor makes obstruction of justice sound innocent.

jokes.text: Oh, well, excuse me, Judge Reinhold, but I am trying to protect my family.
jokes.setup: Michael criticizing his father's illegal activities
jokes.punchline: George Sr. sarcastically compares Michael to the actor Judge Reinhold
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that works because it's randomly specific and dismissive. Shows George Sr.'s sarcastic nature.

jokes.text: Well, it wasn't really your 50th birthday.
jokes.setup: Michael questioning a fake gold necklace
jokes.punchline: George Sr. casually reveals the birthday was also fake
jokes.explanation: Perfect throwaway line showing the family's casual dishonesty. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It's costing us a fortune. He's sleeping us dry.
jokes.setup: Complaining about Buster's fake coma costs
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'bleeding us dry' with sleep-related pun
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that works in context. Shows George Sr.'s focus on money over family.

jokes.text: I wish you would say something. Of course, if you did, everything would be ruined 'cause you wouldn't be as pure.
jokes.setup: Nurse caring for comatose Buster
jokes.punchline: She wants him to speak but also wants him to stay silent to preserve his 'purity'
jokes.explanation: Shows the nurse's contradictory desires and attraction to Buster's helplessness. Good character work.

jokes.text: Just the one who married Lindsay. / Oh, it isn't fun to talk like this?
jokes.setup: Michael asking about N. Bluth again
jokes.punchline: Lucille continues thinking they're both mocking Tobias
jokes.explanation: Good callback maintaining the running gag while showing Lucille's joy in family mockery.

jokes.text: Like Tom here. / I'm 30. / You're kidding me!
jokes.setup: Michael commenting on old equipment still being functional
jokes.punchline: Tom reveals he's only 30, not an old machine
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke. Michael's casual dehumanization of employees played for laughs.

jokes.text: K-Y Pro, huh? Hey, don't get any moisture on these bab... Hey.
jokes.setup: Tobias examining old computer equipment
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about lubricant while warning about moisture damage
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias accidental innuendo. The computer brand name triggers his unconscious sexual commentary while discussing technical equipment.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, we were probably all so jacked up on amyl and disco music, we didn't notice.
jokes.setup: Commenting on old computer speeds
jokes.punchline: Tobias casually mentions being high on amyl nitrate in the disco era
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias - casually revealing wild past while discussing computers. The specificity of 'amyl and disco music' is brilliant.

jokes.text: I was hoping to have a jaw session with you. / Well, why don't we just say--as long as that means a talk.
jokes.setup: Tobias asking to talk
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately clarifies the potentially sexual terminology
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's learned wariness of Tobias's inadvertent double entendres. Good character interaction.

jokes.text: Michael. / Can you believe it? / You're married to my sister. / Don't you think I know that?!
jokes.setup: Tobias saying he's attracted to someone named Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael thinks Tobias means him romantically
jokes.explanation: Excellent confusion comedy. Michael's defensive reaction shows his awareness of Tobias's questionable sexuality.

jokes.text: No, no, did you think you were the Michael I was talking about? / Interesting that your mind went there, though.
jokes.setup: Clarifying the Michael confusion
jokes.punchline: Tobias psychoanalyzes Michael while missing his own obvious implications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias - completely oblivious to his own implications while analyzing others. Great irony.

jokes.text: I'm afraid the Michael I'm talking about is all lady.
jokes.setup: Clarifying which Michael he's attracted to
jokes.punchline: Uses awkward phrasing 'all lady' instead of normal terms
jokes.explanation: Tobias's clinical/awkward way of describing women. The phrasing is distinctly him.

jokes.text: Wise words, Boy Michael.
jokes.setup: Michael gives advice about not calling the trainer
jokes.punchline: Tobias calls him 'Boy Michael' to distinguish from girl Michael
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist solution to the name confusion. 'Boy Michael' is brilliantly clinical and weird.

jokes.text: Hey, coulda, shoulda, woulda. Hey, hey. No, no. Ooh, look, number's up.
jokes.setup: Tobias wanting to give Michael a sweaty hug
jokes.punchline: Michael's increasingly desperate attempts to avoid physical contact
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy as Michael desperately deflects unwanted affection. His relief at the printer finishing is palpable and relatable.

jokes.text: I believe death is wrong in all of its forms. Except lethal injection, but that's just as a deterrent against more death.
jokes.setup: Politician defending life
jokes.punchline: Contradicts himself immediately with exception for state-sanctioned killing
jokes.explanation: Good political satire showing typical pro-life politician hypocrisy. The immediate contradiction is well-timed.

jokes.text: That is unless, of course, it's as a penalty in a three-strike scenario.
jokes.setup: Continuing his pro-life stance
jokes.punchline: Another immediate exception undermining his principles
jokes.explanation: Continues the political hypocrisy theme. Less impactful than the first contradiction.

jokes.text: She grew into the nose.
jokes.setup: Seeing adult Nellie at the bar
jokes.punchline: References his earlier comment about her prominent nose
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing continuity. Michael's shallow focus remains consistent.

jokes.text: Most prostitutes are.
jokes.setup: Noting that Nellie was a great listener
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals why with matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrator timing and economy. The matter-of-fact delivery of dark information is classic AD.

jokes.text: Well, I was buried in loan debt from business school, so I started whoring myself out.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about her career path
jokes.punchline: She literally describes prostitution as 'whoring myself out'
jokes.explanation: Clear setup that Michael completely misses. Her casual honesty about prostitution is funny.

jokes.text: I do all sorts of scenes.
jokes.setup: Michael asking if she does finances
jokes.punchline: She responds in prostitution terms while he thinks business
jokes.explanation: Good double meaning. Shows the sustained misunderstanding between them.

jokes.text: The extensive bleeped-out list of sexual won'ts
jokes.setup: Nellie setting ground rules for work
jokes.punchline: Extremely graphic sexual restrictions that are completely bleeped
jokes.explanation: Great use of censorship for comedy. The extensive bleeping makes it funnier while showing the office confusion.

jokes.text: I bought you a wedding ring... tone.
jokes.setup: George Michael giving Maeby something wedding-related
jokes.punchline: It's just a ringtone, not an actual ring
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing George Michael's attempt to be romantic while staying safe. Good character work.

jokes.text: Nellie has blown them all away. / Yeah, well, I think it's crazy. / You forgot to say 'away' again.
jokes.setup: Michael describing Nellie's work performance
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'blown' in sexual context
jokes.explanation: Excellent double entendre that works on multiple levels. Shows Michael's obliviousness to the obvious.

jokes.text: No, you'd just, you'd go out and you'd just whore it up, Lindsay, you know? Now she's different.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he trusts Nellie over Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Uses 'whore it up' to describe Lindsay while praising actual prostitute
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Michael literally describes Lindsay as a whore while trusting an actual prostitute. Perfect dramatic irony.

jokes.text: It's like we finish each other's... / Sandwiches? / Sentences. / Why would I say...? / Sandwiches? / That time I was going to say sandwiches.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his connection with Nellie
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's random word choice accidentally becomes correct
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and timing. The absurdity of 'sandwiches' becoming correct is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Well, excuse me, Judge Reinhold, but I'm feeling a little pressure here.
jokes.setup: Michael criticizing Lindsay's marriage approach
jokes.punchline: Lindsay uses the same random Judge Reinhold reference as her father
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing family similarity. The random Judge Reinhold reference becomes a running gag.

jokes.text: Let's face it. There's no denying that there's a heat between us. / I'd like to deny that.
jokes.setup: Nellie suggesting attraction between them
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate, emphatic denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing on Michael's denial. His horror at potential incest is played well.

jokes.text: Franklin is Frank? / Mr. F.
jokes.setup: Discovering Franklin puppet's connection to Mr. F
jokes.punchline: Michael realizes the mysterious Mr. F was Gob's puppet all along
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to long-running mystery. The reveal that Mr. F was just Gob's puppet is perfectly absurd and well-earned.

jokes.text: I ain't so little when I take my pants off, lady.
jokes.setup: Nellie commenting on Franklin being 'little'
jokes.punchline: Franklin brags about his size with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency for Franklin's crude personality. The sexual boasting fits the pimp character.

jokes.text: No, I think that's going to be something big.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting this might be Gob's sign from God
jokes.punchline: Gob dismisses it, still expecting a bigger sign
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob - completely missing obvious signs while waiting for something grander. Shows his obliviousness.

jokes.text: I just assumed you wanted a Holy Trinity.
jokes.setup: Revealing why she agreed to meet both of them
jokes.punchline: She also interpreted Holy Trinity as a threesome
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the religious misunderstanding is widespread. Links multiple plot threads.

jokes.text: She's my prostitute. / But sex wasn't one of them. I just want my brother to envy my money, but he's got that hair.
jokes.setup: George Sr. admitting to hiring prostitute
jokes.punchline: Reveals he doesn't even want sex, just wants to make his brother jealous of spending money
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character reveal. George Sr.'s petty sibling rivalry leading to hiring prostitutes just for jealousy is perfectly twisted.

jokes.text: She's a prostitute! That's her job!
jokes.setup: Michael saying Nellie liked him
jokes.punchline: George Sr. pointing out the obvious professional requirement
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment. George Sr. stating the obvious that Michael missed is well-timed.

jokes.text: I make 300 grand a year. / Marry me.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting prostitution isn't a good career
jokes.punchline: Immediately proposes when he learns her income
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing showing Michael's priorities. His instant proposal based on money is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: It's weird on so many levels.
jokes.setup: Michael's marriage proposal to potential sister
jokes.punchline: Nellie's understated reaction to the bizarre situation
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction to absurd situation. Nellie's dry response grounds the comedy.
